title: cyperus subbadius
intro: Cyperus subbadius, also known as the Subbadian papyrus sedge, is a plant species belonging to the sedge family (Cyperaceae). It is native to tropical Africa and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine. The plant has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits, such as wound healing and the treatment of various infections. In herbal preparations, Cyperus subbadius is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and is traditionally consumed to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
